content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Prompting: On-the-Fly Bias Mitigation for Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models often reproduce and amplify social biases and stereotypes present in their training data. Current mitigation approaches rely on pre-defined bias detection and post-processing techniques, which have limited adaptability and coverage.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are deployed in increasingly sensitive domains, it is crucial to reduce harmful outputs related to gender, race, and other social factors. Prompt-based interventions are agile and do not require model retraining, but most focus on direct steering rather than critical self-reflection. A dynamic, context-aware prompting framework can help models self-identify and neutralize biases at inference time.",
  "method": "We propose a novel 'Counterfactual Prompting' technique that, for each input, automatically generates minimally perturbed counterfactual prompts—altering key social variables (e.g., gender, ethnicity) in the prompt—and asks the model to compare its own responses. A meta-prompt then instructs the model to identify inconsistencies or stereotypes in its original response versus the counterfactuals and to revise its answer to minimize such bias. This pipeline leverages the model's own generative and reasoning capacities for bias self-correction, without external classifiers.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate our method on social bias benchmarks such as StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs, using standard bias metrics (e.g., stereotype score, language modeling accuracy). Baselines include original model outputs, direct instruction prompts, and output filtering. Human evaluation will assess both bias reduction and coherence of generated responses."
}
